message:
test(safe_channel): cover query methods, close-wake, and metrics lifecycle; sync Cargo.lock

crates/frankenterm-core/src/safe_channel.rs gains three focused unit
tests pinning behavior that previously had no direct coverage:

  - query_methods_and_reservation_accessors walks the channel through
    empty, one-item, and one-reservation states and asserts every
    observer surface: Sender::len/is_empty/is_closed,
    Receiver::pending_reservations/is_drained,
    Reservation::id/peek/into_inner. It also locks in the contract
    that the first reservation from a fresh channel receives
    ReservationId(0) (fetch_add starts at 0), that into_inner is an
    alias for commit, and that is_drained is false as long as either
    the queue is non-empty or a reservation is outstanding but
    becomes true after close.
  - close_wakes_blocked_on_reservation_limit builds a channel whose
    max_reservations is 1, fills the slot, and then spawns a thread
    that calls reserve() with an empty queue. The only wake path
    available is the reservation_released condvar; closing the
    sender must deliver Err(SafeChannelError::Closed) to the blocked
    reserver rather than deadlocking. This regression-guards the
    close-vs-limit-wait race.
  - metrics_lifecycle_accuracy drives the full counter surface
    through send → reserve → commit → rollback → drop-rollback →
    send failure, asserting total_sent, total_committed,
    total_rollbacks, total_drop_rollbacks, queue_depth, queue_hwm,
    reserved_count, reservation_hwm, and total_send_failures each
    transition as documented. In particular it locks in that the
    queue high-water mark is preserved after commits drain the
    queue, and that dropping a Reservation without commit/rollback
    increments total_drop_rollbacks.

Cargo.lock absorbs the dev-dependency drift from the recent series of
proptest-based test additions (bintree, rangeset, dynamic, luahelper,
ratelim, promise, termwiz-serde, umask, open-url, mux/serde_json,
etc.) whose manifest edits already landed on main in commits
2e734092..6a971051. No source code changes here beyond the lockfile
update and the safe_channel test additions above.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
